prompt: Assess our SAFe implementation. We have 3 ARTs running Essential SAFe. PI predictability averages 65%, and teams report that PI Planning feels like a formality rather than a genuine planning event.
assertions: Must evaluate ART health metrics including the 65% PI predictability as below target
Must assess PI Planning quality and identify why it feels ceremonial rather than valuable
Must analyze whether Essential SAFe core practices are properly implemented
Must recommend specific improvements to increase PI predictability toward the 80-100% target

prompt: Our organization is moving from Essential SAFe to Full SAFe. We have 5 ARTs and want to add portfolio-level practices. Assess our readiness for this transition.
assertions: Must evaluate current Essential SAFe maturity as a foundation for Full SAFe
Must assess lean portfolio management readiness including funding model and epic flow
Must identify gaps in organizational agility and continuous learning culture
Must provide a phased roadmap for transitioning to Full SAFe configuration

prompt: Evaluate our PI execution. Last 3 PIs show declining objective achievement (85%, 72%, 61%) and increasing dependency conflicts between teams.
assertions: Must analyze the declining PI objective achievement trend and root causes
Must evaluate dependency management practices during PI Planning and execution
Must assess whether PI objectives are properly SMART and confidence votes are genuine
Must recommend corrective actions to reverse the declining performance trend

prompt: Our lean portfolio management is struggling. Epics take 6+ months to go from Funnel to Implementation, and business stakeholders complain that SAFe slows everything down.
assertions: Must analyze portfolio Kanban flow and identify bottleneck stages causing the 6-month cycle time
Must evaluate whether lean business cases are appropriately lightweight or overly bureaucratic
Must assess whether WSJF or another prioritization framework is effectively applied
Must address the perception that SAFe creates overhead rather than enabling agility
